title: SARIF AI emit profile — finalized whole log
description: Whole-document contract for a fully-enriched SARIF AI log: the output of the emit-finalize verb. An overlay on the canonical SARIF 2.1.0 document that reuses the base shape by $ref and tightens it to the Error-level AI rules at whole-log scale. Unlike the per-object input schemas, this is a POST-enrichment contract: it ACCEPTS the index/identity state finalize assigns (result.ruleIndex, result.guid, artifactLocation.index, run.taxonomies) and REQUIRES the run-scoped fields the AI profile mandates (tool.driver.name, a versionControlProvenance entry unless the run is stamped properties[unpublishable]=true by emit-finalize --no-repo, properties[ai/origin]) plus, when a run carries the Azure DevOps pipeline shape, the GHAzDO automationDetails contract (GHAzDO1019/GHAzDO1020). What this schema cannot express at whole-log fidelity (e.g. the full org/project/buildId segment structure of automationDetails.id) is deferred to emit-finalize --validate.

aiLogRun.description: A finalized AI run. Reuses the base run by $ref and tightens it to the AI profile: a named tool driver, properties[ai/origin], a results array (which finalize always writes, possibly empty), and — unless the run is stamped properties[unpublishable]=true by emit-finalize --no-repo — at least one versionControlProvenance entry. Header-only forbids from the input profile are lifted here — results live in the whole log.

else.description: A publishable run must carry version-control provenance; a run finalized --no-repo (properties[unpublishable]=true) is exempt.

informationUri.description: When present, a canonical lowercase https URI.

versionControlProvenance.description: At least one version-control provenance entry. Each repositoryUri, when present, is a canonical lowercase https URI.

uri.description: Canonical lowercase https or file URI.

properties.description: ai/origin is required and identifies how the run was produced.

results.description: The finalized result set. May be empty (a clean scan), but each result present must satisfy the AI result contract.

aiLogResult.description: A finalized AI result. Reuses the base result by $ref and tightens it to the Error-level AI rules (ruleId grammar, message.markdown, region). Unlike ai-result.schema.json (the producer input), this ACCEPTS the index/identity state finalize assigns: ruleIndex, guid, and artifactLocation.index are permitted here.

ruleId.description: A finalized result.ruleId. Three accepted shapes: a CWE sub-id 'CWE-<number>/<sub-id>' (e.g. CWE-89/kql-injection-from-config); the NOVEL escape hatch 'NOVEL-<sub-id>'; or a BARE collapsed taxonomy id 'CWE-<number>' (e.g. CWE-89). The bare form is the post-collapse shape emit-finalize writes for GitHub-hosted runs, where the sub-id is folded to its descriptor id so GitHub's ruleId-string rule binding still matches. This is the one place the finalized (output) grammar is a SUPERSET of the per-object input grammar, which rejects the bare form. The host-conditional restriction (bare form legal only on a GitHub-hosted run) is not expressible at schema fidelity and is deferred to AI1012 / emit-finalize --validate.

ruleIndex.description: Finalize-assigned rule index (>= 0). Producers do not author this; the replayer assigns it.

message.description: message.markdown is required and must contain non-whitespace.

locations.description: At least one location. A location bearing a physicalLocation must carry a region whose startLine is an integer >= 1.

aiLogAutomationDetails.description: Finalized run.automationDetails. When present, guid/correlationGuid (if supplied) must be canonical GUIDs. A run that carries the Azure DevOps pipeline shape — detected by an azuredevops/pipeline/build/* property key or an automationDetails.id bearing that prefix — must satisfy the full GHAzDO contract: GHAzDO1020 (id starts with 'azuredevops/pipeline/build/') and GHAzDO1019 (all four pipeline properties, well-typed). A non-pipeline AI run may carry an automationDetails with only guid/correlationGuid and is not held to the GHAzDO contract.

then.description: GHAzDO1020 + GHAzDO1019: the pipeline shape requires a prefixed id and all four well-typed pipeline properties.

azuredevops/pipeline/build/phaseId.description: Canonical GUID != the empty GUID, serialized as a string.
